---
title: "Resources"
toc: false
---

Readings and outside links. The goal is a short, opinionated list — not
a dump.

## Optional / supplemental texts

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
### *Introductory Statistics for the Life and Biomedical Sciences*

- **Publisher / source:** OpenIntro
- **License:** Creative Commons BY-SA 3.0
- **Where to get it:** the official OpenIntro project page

<!--
TODO (resources): confirm and link the canonical OpenIntro ISLBS page
(URL likely under https://openintro.org/book/ but not verified from
local source files yet) and add the canonical author list.  Authors
were not recorded in our local planning notes; do not list them until
verified from the official OpenIntro page.
-->

Used as an **optional supplemental reading** for biomedical and
life-science framing in later units (probability as risk, diagnostic
testing, categorical outcomes).  Adaptations of material from this
book that appear in our course will carry the appropriate CC BY-SA
3.0 attribution.
:::

## Planned sections (placeholders)

- A short list of readings worth doing
- StatKey quick-start (used for simulation later in the semester)
- Where to find clean public datasets
- How to read a graph / a table / a regression output

More entries will be added as the course is built.
